questions.question: Find the slope of the line passing through the points (–2, 5) and (4, –7).
questions.explanation: Use the slope formula: m = (y₂ – y₁)/(x₂ – x₁) = (–7 – 5)/(4 – (–2)) = –12/6 = –2.

questions.question: A line has slope 3 and passes through the point (2, 1). Write the equation in slope-intercept form.
questions.explanation: Use point-slope form: y – 1 = 3(x – 2). Expand: y – 1 = 3x – 6. Solve for y: y = 3x – 5.

questions.question: What is the slope of the line given by the equation 2x – 5y = 10?
questions.explanation: Rearrange to slope-intercept form: –5y = –2x + 10, so y = (2/5)x – 2. The slope is the coefficient of x.

questions.question: Two parallel lines have the same slope. If one line passes through (1, 3) with slope –1/2, what is the y-intercept of a parallel line passing through (4, 5)?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines share slope –1/2. Use y = mx + b: 5 = (–1/2)(4) + b gives 5 = –2 + b, so b = 7.

questions.question: A cable car travels 180 meters up a hill in 60 seconds. If it continues at this constant rate, how many seconds will it take to travel 450 meters?
questions.explanation: The rate (slope) is 180/60 = 3 meters per second. Time = distance/rate = 450/3 = 150 seconds.

questions.question: The line through points (–1, k) and (3, 7) has a slope of 2. Find the value of k.
questions.explanation: Set up the slope equation: (7 – k)/(3 – (–1)) = 2. Simplify: (7 – k)/4 = 2, so 7 – k = 8, giving k = –1.
